specific_location: NW of the boat ramp at Barr Lake SP.
description: This adult male was bright blue-green on back and head with dark wings and all white under. It was smaller than a near Killdeer. It swooped and dived and flew just like a Tree Swallow. I have observed them many times in the mountains where they nest, so I know them well.Behaviors: It swooped over a group of weeds on the frozen lake several times like it was trying to gleen insects from the weeds.Call: None heard.Plumage: Adult Male
photographer: Photographer: Gregg Goodrich, Gregg Goodrich, Gregg Goodrich
